objective: When a task ends with status implementation_failed or error, write a structured failure record to .glitchlab/failures.jsonl. Each record should include: task_id, timestamp, objective (first 100 chars), steps_taken, files_read (list), files_edited (list), last_tool_called, failure_reason (from the result dict or exception message), and model_used. At pipeline startup, if the current task objective matches a previous failure (fuzzy substring match on the first 60 chars), inject the failure record into the planner's user message as "Previous attempt context" so the planner can adjust its plan accordingly. Read the failure file in the controller, not in the agents.

plan_steps.description: Update the controller to read .glitchlab/failures.jsonl at startup, match the objective, and inject previous failure context into the planner. Also, add logic at the end of the task execution to write a failure record to .glitchlab/failures.jsonl if the task fails.

changelog_entry:
### Added
- **Failure Context Injection**: The controller now records failed task attempts to `.glitchlab/failures.jsonl`. If a new task objective matches a previous failure, the failure context (steps taken, files read/edited, reason) is automatically injected into the planner's prompt to prevent repeating mistakes.

data.description: Tests that failed tasks are recorded in .glitchlab/failures.jsonl with the correct fields, and that subsequent tasks with similar objectives receive the failure context.
